_note: Chapter 11 — the siege of the Factory. Spot -> wire -> secure -> loose the Judge -> hold -> Molly arrives.

c11_catwalk.name: High Catwalk — Dawn
c11_catwalk.desc: First light the color of solder, and out on the Solitude: hovers in convoy formation, fanning wide the way professionals fan when they've bought the map in advance. They've come for the gray box. They've budgeted for everyone who knows it exists.

c11_loft.name: Gentry's Loft — War Room
c11_loft.desc: Gentry has stopped being fragile. Every screen carries a sightline now; the Factory's dead wiring is waking up under his hands, floodlights and fence current and things you welded years ago for reasons you lost. 'They want my Shape,' he says. 'They may not HAVE it.'

c11_ground.name: Factory — Ground Floor
c11_ground.desc: Torchlight on steel and everything that cuts, rolled to the doors. The Judge stands finished in the center of the floor — two and a half meters of blades and verdicts — waiting for the only permission it's ever wanted.

interactions.label: Assign the machines their fields of fire
pages: The Witch is fast and cannot distinguish surrender from a change in posture. The Corpsegrinder is slow, loud, and nearly impossible to stop once its flywheel catches. The Judge understands six commands and interprets all of them as escalation.

You place each where its failure will hurt the Factory least. This is what separates sculpture from defense: not beauty, not intent, but the obligation to imagine what your work does after control is lost.

c11_bench.name: The Count's Corner — Secured
c11_bench.desc: You and Cherry drag plate steel into a redoubt around the stretcher. The Count dreams on, oblivious, his universe humming in its gray brick. Cherry checks his lines, then checks the shotgun you gave her, in that order. 'He stays breathing,' she says. It's not a question.

c11_marvins.name: Marvin's — Parts & Salvage
c11_marvins.desc: Marvin sells to both sides of every trouble on the Solitude, which is why he's still alive, and today he's selling to you at panic prices with the shutters half down. 'Whatever's coming,' he says, weighing your credit, 'buy it now.'

interactions.label: Trace who bought battle parts before you
pages: Marvin's empty bins tell the story. Someone bought directional microphones, cold-weather seals, and replacement skirts for military hovers two days ago. They paid through six accounts and asked for a road map no local would need.

One requisition includes cutting wheels sized to your machines. The attackers studied the Factory as an obstacle and purchased tools for dismantling your art. Marvin knew enough to raise prices and not enough to warn you. On Dog Solitude, neutrality is another word for invoicing both funerals.

c11_yard.name: The Yard — Contact
c11_yard.desc: The Judge takes the first hover the way it took your nightmares: entirely. The Witch spins wire and murder through the second. You work the remote with both hands, and for once the lost time works FOR you — you are all reflex, all art, and the yard is your gallery opening.
c11_yard.locked_text: Not into the open yet — Gentry's still wiring the yard. Go up and help him first.

c11_perimeter.name: Perimeter — Aftermath
c11_perimeter.desc: Burned hulls smoking on the flats, the convoy's discipline gone the way of its paymaster's budget. And out of the north, unannounced: one battered car, driving like physics owes it money, headlights flashing an old cowboy code Gentry says means DON'T SHOOT, YOU IDIOTS.
c11_perimeter.locked_text: The Judge hasn't spoken yet. Nothing out there is finished being dangerous.

interactions.label: Read the battle in its wreckage
pages: The convoy expected frightened squatters and one eccentric technician. Their ammunition is specialized, their armor corporate, their retreat plan nonexistent. Whoever hired them priced secrecy above survival.

Near the first burned hover lies a guidance beacon keyed to the aleph's emissions. The box was never hidden once it began drawing power. Gentry's Shape hunt made the Factory visible; Bobby's universe bent the world around itself until money, ghosts, gods, and guns all rolled downhill toward Dog Solitude.
